steps.title: Apply the quadratic formula
steps.content:
The equation is $$4x^{2}+5x-5=0$$ with $$a=4$$, $$b=5$$, $$c=-5$$.
Compute the discriminant: $$b^{2}-4ac = 5^{2}-4(4)(-5)=25+80=105$$.
Then the solutions are $$x= rac{-5\pm\sqrt{105}}{2\cdot4}= rac{-5\pm\sqrt{105}}{8}$$.

steps.title: Select the greatest solution
steps.content:
The two solutions are $$ rac{-5-\sqrt{105}}{8}$$ and $$ rac{-5+\sqrt{105}}{8}$$; the larger one uses the plus sign.
Thus the greatest solution is $$ rac{-5+\sqrt{105}}{8}$$. That matches choice B — B is correct.
